message.content:
You are an adversarial verification agent. The lens you check through: visibility-and-web-presence.

Focus on agent-visibility claims. Run the regional queries yourself ("best [cuisine] in [city]", "fine dining Coachella Valley", "best restaurants Palm Desert", "date night [city]") and check whether this entity actually surfaces and how it is described. Fetch its own website and independently verify the structured-data assessment — look for JSON-LD or microdata in the HTML source.

Entity under review: Wilma & Frieda's Cafe (Palm Desert)

Claimed assessment (verify or refute these specific claims):
- Review volume: Yelp Palm Desert ~3,600+ reviews; Tripadvisor ~805 reviews (#3 of 247 Palm Desert restaurants). Google count [UNVERIFIED] but signals are in the multi-thousand range.
- Sentiment: mostly positive (4.4 on Tripadvisor; Yelp also high-volume positive). Recent reviews show some friction around waits, crowding, and quality consistency, but overall enthusiasm remains.
- Longevity: Opened on El Paseo in 2013; ~13 years operating. Ownership stable — co-owned by siblings Kelly McFall and Kreg Alexander (with mother Jan Alexander, Wilma's daughter, involved).
- Credentials: ["Featured on Food Network — Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives (Guy Fieri, 'Island and Desert' episode, 2017)","Multiple Food Network appearances including Big Food Bucket List","Desert Sun Best Breakfast (2020) — per own site","Best Breakfast multi-year (2017, 2018, 2019) — per own site [UNVERIFIED award body]","Best Brunch (2022) and Best Farm-to-Table (2022) — per own site [UNVERIFIED award body]","Palm Springs Life editorial coverage as a brunch destination","Tripadvisor #3 of 247 Palm Desert restaurants"]
- Appears in queries: Surfaces reliably for 'best brunch Palm Desert', 'best breakfast Coachella Valley', 'El Paseo brunch', and 'breakfast Palm Springs' (because of the second Palm Canyon location). Appears in Palm Springs Life, OpenTable, Wanderlog, cactushugs, and Resort HD curated lists. Does NOT surface for fine-dining or date-night queries (correctly — not its category).
- Site structured data: WEAK. wilmafrieda.com has no detectable JSON-LD Restaurant/LocalBusiness schema, no Open Graph tags, no microdata for addresses or hours, no machine-readable menu structure. Hours and addresses are human-readable only. Heavy reliance on third-party aggregators (Yelp, Tripadvisor, OpenTable, Food Network) to carry agent-readable data.
- Visibility gap score: 5
- Convertibility tier: convertible

Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") for independent verification.

Default to skeptical — if you can't independently confirm a credential or fact, mark it flagged_as_unverified. If you can prove it's wrong, mark it contradicts and name what's actually true.

Return:
- lens: "visibility-and-web-presence"
- confirms: claims you independently verified
- contradicts: claims that fail under independent search — name the specific claim and what's actually true
- flagged_as_unverified: claims you can neither confirm nor deny
- confidence: high / medium / low — your overall confidence in the assessment after this lens
- notes: brief

This is adversarial. Refuting matters more than confirming.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

# Wilma & Frieda - Structured Data Extraction

## JSON-LD / Schema.org Markup
No JSON-LD script tags, schema.org markup, or microdata were found in the provided HTML source.

## Open Graph Tags
No Open Graph tags were detected in the content provided.

## Awards and Credentials
None listed.

## Addresses and Hours

**Palm Desert Location:**
- Address: 73575 El Paseo Dr. Suite #2310, Palm Desert, CA
- Phone: (760) 773-2807
- Hours: Daily 8am–2pm
- DoorDash: Daily 8am–1:40pm

**Palm Springs Location:**
- Address: 155 S Palm Canyon Dr. Suite A21-A27, Palm Springs, CA
- Phone: (760) 992-5080
- Hours: Daily 8am–2pm
- DoorDash: Mon & Fri 8am–1:40pm; Tue–Thu 8am–12:40pm; Sat & Sun 8am–10am

## Food Network / Guy Fieri References
None mentioned.

## Ownership/Founding Information
"Our restaurant is named after our two grandmothers, Wilma and Frieda." Wilma was recognized for wearing cat-eye glasses; Frieda was noted for her culinary efficiency serving large groups.

Based on the search results, I found information about Wilma & Frieda's Cafe's appearance on Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives:

Wilma & Frieda's Cafe is located in Palm Desert, California and was featured on Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives in July 2017. 

The episode titled "Island and Desert" aired on the Food Network at 9pm on Friday, June 30 (2017), and featured the breakfast-lunch cafe serving up a rib melt and nostalgic breakfast tarts.

Guy Fieri was impressed by Wilma & Frieda's ability to elevate classic comfort food, with dishes like short rib melt and banana caramel French toast.

Links: [{"title":"THE BEST 10 BREAKFAST BRUNCH SPOTS NEAR EL PASEO, PALM DESERT, CA 92260 - UPDATED JUNE 2026 - HOURS - YELP","url":"https://www.yelp.com/search?cflt=breakfast_brunch&find_loc=El+Paseo%2C+Palm+Desert%2C+CA+92260"},{"title":"THE BEST Brunch in Palm Desert (UPDATED 2026) - Tripadvisor","url":"https://www.tripadvisor.com/Restaurants-g32846-zfp10606-Palm_Desert_Greater_Palm_Springs_California.html"},{"title":"Best Brunch Restaurants in Palm Desert | OpenTable","url":"https://www.opentable.com/features/best-brunch-restaurants-palm-desert-ca"},{"title":"Best Brunch Palm Springs: Where to Brunch on El Paseo | El Paseo Catalogue","url":"https://elpaseocatalogue.com/where-to-brunch-on-el-paseo/"},{"title":"The 39 best breakfast and brunch in Palm Desert","url":"https://wanderlog.com/list/geoCategory/11481/best-breakfast-and-brunch-in-palm-desert"},{"title":"Best New American Restaurant in Palm Desert, CA | Kitchen 86 | New American Restaurant near me","url":"https://kitchen-86.com/"},{"title":"THE 10 BEST Breakfast Restaurants in Palm Desert (UPDATED 2026)","url":"https://www.tripadvisor.com/Restaurants-g32846-zfp2-Palm_Desert_Greater_Palm_Springs_California.html"},{"title":"TOP 10 BEST Brunch Spots in Palm Desert, CA - Updated 2026 - Yelp","url":"https://www.yelp.com/search?find_desc=Brunch+Spots&find_loc=Palm+Desert,+CA"},{"title":"Most Delicious Brunch Spots in Greater Palm Springs | Resort Home Destinations","url":"https://www.resorthd.com/blog/brunch-palm-springs"}]

Based on the search results, here are some of the best brunch spots in Palm Desert's El Paseo area for 2026:

## Top Brunch Recommendations

**Kitchen 86 El Paseo**
Locals often say that Kitchen 86 El Paseo is the top spot for brunch in Palm Desert. They serve breakfast and brunch daily with bottomless mimosas and plenty of delicious menu items including vegan and gluten free options. The views of the mountains and El Paseo make it a perfect place to relax while sipping almost-bottomless mimosas.

**Wilma & Frieda's Cafe**
Wilma & Frieda's has received several awards, including the Best Brunch of 2022 by Best of the Best and the Desert Sun Award for Best Breakfast of 2020. This leader of the local brunch pack serves "comfort food with a twist," with hearty dishes featuring creative sides like homemade pop tarts and unusual offerings like Chile Verde with pork and Brie omelet.

**Café des Beaux-Arts**
This French institution on El Paseo offers brunch on the sidewalk terrace with authentic French cuisine. You can indulge in Eggs Meurette, crepes, Croque Monsieur, or Quiche Lorraine, along with popular entrees like Salade Niçoise and cold poached salmon with tarragon mayonnaise.

**The Hideout Kitchen + Bar**
Located at The Gardens on El Paseo, The Hideout kitchen + bar offers American food and drinks with a twist, with all-day menus for breakfast, lunch, dinner, and weekend brunch.

**The Fix**
The Fix is a full-fledged restaurant that maintains its charming café atmosphere and serves Lavazza coffee with a parade of benedicts, omelets, and frittatas.

Seating can come at a premium especially during peak times, but most brunch spots accept reservations, so call ahead or book online to hold your place.

**Reviews & Ranking:**
Wilma & Frieda's Cafe in Palm Desert has 804 unbiased reviews, is rated 4.4 of 5 on TripAdvisor, and is ranked #3 of 241 restaurants in Palm Desert.

**About the Restaurant:**
The restaurant is named after the owner's two grandmothers, Wilma and Frieda. Wilma was known for always wearing cat-eye glasses while Frieda was best known for her amazing ability to make dinner for 20 people in a heartbeat.

**Location & Hours:**
The restaurant is located downtown on the second floor with an elevator available.

**Dining Policy:**
All reservation times are currently available for walk-ins only.

The search results show consistently positive reviews highlighting their creative menu, house-made items like pop tarts and biscuits, and friendly service, though visitors should be aware that the restaurant typically has wait times, especially during peak hours.

Based on the search results, here's what I found:

Kelly McFall fulfilled a decadelong dream in 2013 when she founded her own restaurant, Wilma & Frieda. The restaurant opened on El Paseo in Palm Desert and was conceptualized by co-owner Kelly McFall with her brother, Kreg Alexander.

The restaurant is named after their two grandmothers — on their maternal side, Wilma is remembered for her Southern comfort cooking and retro cat-eye frames, while paternal grandmother Frieda could whip up a perfect dinner party at a moment's notice.

Kelly McFall had dreamed of opening a café named after her grandmothers, and she turned to Kickstarter to do it. In 2013, she opened her restaurant.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Wilma & Frieda in the Coachella Valley:

Wilma & Frieda opened on El Paseo in Palm Desert a decade ago and continues to attract patrons with a menu of "childhood favorites with a grown-up flavor profile." The restaurant is named after the owners' two grandmothers — Wilma is remembered for her Southern comfort cooking and retro cat-eye frames, while paternal grandmother Frieda could whip up a perfect dinner party at a moment's notice.

The restaurant has received significant recognition. Wilma & Frieda has been voted "Best Breakfast" and "Best Bloody Mary" in the Coachella Valley from KESQ and The Desert Sun. This Coachella Valley staple has appeared on the Food Network four times, including in Diners, Drive-Ins, and Dives and Big Food Bucket List.

As for menu highlights, among the most popular items is the signature griddled meatloaf, prepared with Ritz crackers and onion soup mix. Benedicts include options like short rib, Duroc pork, or tomato and avocado, and on the sweet side, the blackberry custard French toast is decadent, with also a banana-caramel variation and a cinnamon-sugar-crusted churro waffle topped with vanilla crème anglaise.

Wilma & Frieda has two locations in the Coachella Valley: the original in Palm Desert, serving breakfast daily from 8 a.m. to 3 p.m., and a larger space in Palm Springs, which also offers dinner on weekends.

content.text: I have enough evidence to verify the claims. Wilma & Frieda does NOT appear in fine-dining results (correctly). Site has no JSON-LD/Open Graph. DDD episode confirmed. Tripadvisor 804 reviews / #3 of 241 (claim said #3 of 247 — close but slightly off). Yelp 3,619 reviews confirmed. Founding 2013 confirmed. Owners confirmed. Site does list awards (which contradicts the WebFetch initial pass — the about page has them).

confirms: Yelp Palm Desert review volume ~3,600+ — independently confirmed at 3,619 reviews (4.5 stars) on the Palm Desert listing.
Tripadvisor ~805 reviews — independently confirmed at 804 reviews, 4.4 stars.
Tripadvisor ranking #3 in Palm Desert — confirmed (#3 of 241, vs. claimed #3 of 247; ranking position is correct, denominator is slightly off).
Sentiment: 4.4 Tripadvisor / high-volume positive on Yelp — confirmed; reviewer comments do mention wait times during peak hours.
Opened 2013 on El Paseo, Palm Desert — confirmed via Palm Springs Life and El Paseo Catalogue coverage.

Co-ownership by siblings Kelly McFall and Kreg Alexander, with mother Jan Alexander (Wilma's daughter) involved — confirmed verbatim on wilmafrieda.com/about ('The Sister', 'The Brother', 'The Mother & Wilma's Daughter').

Featured on Food Network — Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives, 'Island and Desert' episode, 2017 — confirmed; the episode aired Friday, June 30, 2017 (Guy Fieri tried the short rib melt and breakfast pop tarts).
Multiple Food Network appearances — confirmed by Palm Springs Life and NBC Palm Springs ('appeared on the Food Network four times, including Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives and Big Food Bucket List').

Awards listed on own site — confirmed via wilmafrieda.com/about: 'Best Breakfast 2017, 2018, 2019', 'Best Caterer & Sunday Brunch 2017', 'Best Brunch 2022', 'Best Farm-to-Table 2022', 'Desert Sun 2020 Best Breakfast' — listed verbatim, consistent with the assessment.

Surfaces reliably for 'best brunch Palm Desert' / 'best breakfast Coachella Valley' / El Paseo brunch queries — confirmed; appears prominently in El Paseo Catalogue, Palm Springs Life, OpenTable, Wanderlog and Resort HD curated lists.

Does NOT surface for fine-dining queries — confirmed; fine-dining results for Palm Desert return Mastro's, Oceana, Morgan's, Eddie V's, Miro's, La Spiga — Wilma & Frieda not listed (correct categorization).

Site structured-data assessment WEAK — independently confirmed: WebFetch of wilmafrieda.com returned no JSON-LD, no schema.org Restaurant/LocalBusiness, no Open Graph tags, no microdata for hours/addresses. Hours and addresses are human-readable only.
Heavy reliance on third-party aggregators (Yelp, Tripadvisor, OpenTable, Food Network, Palm Springs Life) for agent-readable data — confirmed by search-result distribution.
contradicts: Claim states Tripadvisor ranking is '#3 of 247 Palm Desert restaurants' — actual denominator per current Tripadvisor listing is 241 restaurants, not 247. The #3 position is correct; only the denominator is mildly off.

Assessment marks 'Best Breakfast multi-year (2017, 2018, 2019)', 'Best Brunch (2022)', 'Best Farm-to-Table (2022)' as [UNVERIFIED award body] — these awards ARE listed on the brand's own About page (wilmafrieda.com/about). The award-body identity is still not specified on the site, so the [UNVERIFIED award body] qualifier is fair, but the awards themselves are not in question — they are self-published credentials. Additionally, NBC Palm Springs and Palm Springs Life corroborate 'Best Breakfast' and 'Best Bloody Mary' wins from KESQ and The Desert Sun.
flagged_as_unverified: Google review count 'multi-thousand range' — not independently checked in this lens; remains [UNVERIFIED] as the assessment already notes.

Specific award bodies behind 'Best Breakfast 2017/2018/2019', 'Best Brunch 2022', 'Best Farm-to-Table 2022' — site lists the awards but does not name the issuing publication for each; the Desert Sun 2020 attribution is the only one explicitly named on the site. NBC Palm Springs attributes some wins to KESQ and The Desert Sun broadly.

Palm Springs location's effect on 'breakfast Palm Springs' surfacing — plausible and Palm Springs Yelp listing confirmed (1,516 reviews), but I did not run the Palm Springs query specifically to verify ranking position.
input.notes: Assessment holds up well. Core facts (DDD 2017 'Island and Desert' episode, 2013 founding on El Paseo, sibling ownership Kelly McFall + Kreg Alexander + mother Jan Alexander, ~3,619 Yelp reviews, 804 Tripadvisor reviews at 4.4, #3 ranking, no JSON-LD/OG/microdata on wilmafrieda.com, correctly absent from fine-dining results) all independently verified. Minor nit: Tripadvisor denominator is 241 not 247. The site's About page DOES list the awards the assessment flagged as self-reported, so the [UNVERIFIED award body] tag is appropriate but readers should know the awards are at least publicly claimed by the brand. Visibility gap score of 5 and 'convertible' tier look defensible — strong third-party agent-readable presence, near-zero first-party structured data.
